title: No-Op Scope Manager Flow
description: No-op tracing path: all ScopeManager operations return empty/null stubs when tracing is disabled
activity_flow: Caller invokes NoopScopeManager::create() → returns new NoopScopeManager instance
→ NoopScope::create() called → returns new NoopScope (no-op stub)
→ NoopScope returned to caller (span ignored, finishSpanOnClose ignored)
→ NoopScope::create() called → returns new NoopScope (no-op stub)
→ method body is empty (no-op) → returns void
Caller optionally calls NoopScope::close() → empty no-op, void
Caller optionally calls NoopScope::getSpan() → NoopSpan::create() → returns new NoopSpan stub
key_relationships: NoopScopeManager depends_on NoopScope (creates via static factory)
NoopScope depends_on NoopSpan (getSpan() creates via static factory)
NoopTracer depends_on NoopScopeManager (getScopeManager() returns new instance)
NoopTracer depends_on NoopScope (startActiveSpan, startRootSpan, getRootScope all return NoopScope::create())
important_notes: NoopScopeManager is ported from opentracing/opentracing-php reference implementation
All methods are intentional no-ops: activate() and getActive() always return a fresh NoopScope (never null), close() does nothing
NoopTracer::getScopeManager() returns new NoopScopeManager() — not a singleton
DEFAULT_FINISH_SPAN_ON_CLOSE=true is defined on the interface but has no effect in no-op implementation
NoopScope::getSpan() returns a fresh NoopSpan (also a no-op stub) — no stored state
The no-op path is activated when DDTrace is disabled or limited (NoopTracer::limited() returns true)
